title: Template-Driven Forms
content:
### What are Template-Driven Forms? Template-driven forms rely on directives in the template to build and manage forms. Angular infers the form model from the DOM structure. They are simpler but less scalable than reactive forms. ### Setup Import `FormsModule`: ```typescript import { FormsModule } from '@angular/forms'; @NgModule({ imports: [FormsModule] }) export class AppModule { } ``` ### Basic Form ```html
``` - **`ngForm`** — Angular automatically creates a `FormGroup` on `
` elements. The `#loginForm="ngForm"` template reference gives access to the form state. - **`ngModel`** — Creates a `FormControl` for the input. Must have a `name` attribute. - **`ngSubmit`** — Event fired on form submission. ### Two-Way Binding with ngModel ```html ``` This binds the input value directly to the component property. ### ngModelGroup Group related controls: ```html
``` ### Form State Classes Angular automatically applies CSS classes based on control state: | Class | Description | |-------|-------------| | `ng-valid` / `ng-invalid` | Validation status | | `ng-touched` / `ng-untouched` | Whether the control has been blurred | | `ng-dirty` / `ng-pristine` | Whether the control value has changed | | `ng-pending` | Async validation in progress | ```html

Email is required.

``` ### Accessing Form State ```html
...

Form valid: {{ form.valid }}

Form submitted: {{ form.submitted }}

Form touched: {{ form.touched }}

Form dirty: {{ form.dirty }}

```
codeExample:
Name is required
Valid email required

quiz.question: Which module must be imported for template-driven forms?
What does the `ngForm` directive do on a `<form>` element?
options: Creates a FormGroup and tracks form state
Binds the form to a service
